title: Daclif Gallamby
content:

{{Quote|Please be courteous to the Diktat. He's a powerful man.|[[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mack Jasper|Jasper]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:StDO">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Search the Diktat's Office|Corellia}}</ref>}}
'''Daclif Gallamby''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] statesman who was remembered as the last [[Diktat]] of the [[Corellian republic]] and was [[Governor-General/Legends|Governor-General]] of the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] following the death of his predecessor, [[Dupas Thomree]], in the first days of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. With the Corellian sector becoming part of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Gallamby was widely considered as a puppet with no real power. Nevertheless, Daclif Gallamby presided Corellia more than a decade, during a period that was troubled by the escalation of the war between the Galactic Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Hence, his rule was marked by increasing insecurity and isolation of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] toward the rest of the galaxy, resulting in the decline of the Corellian economy. 

After the defeat of the Empire during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Gallamby lost the protection and therefore attempted to prevent the fall of his government. Finally, his rule collapsed in response to the vehement protests of the Corellian people who forced Gallamby to flee Corellia.

==Biography==
===Diktat of Corellia===
{{Quote|I think Thomree figured he had protected himself from assassination by makin' a nobody his successor. Wouldn't be the first time someone tried that—or the first time it failed.|[[Thrackan Sal-Solo]]|<ref name="AaS" />}}
During the early [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], Daclif Gallamby served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-backed Corellian government led by [[Diktat]] [[Dupas Thomree]]. When Thomree was assassinated in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], he was replaced by his designated successor, Gallamby, who up until then was unknown to the public.<ref name="AaS" />

[[File:Corellia_SOF.png|thumb|left|220px|Corellia, seat of power of the Diktat]]
As the Diktat of Corellia, Daclif Gallamby was responsible for the direct administration of the planet.<ref name="SoF">''[[Suns of Fortune]]''</ref> He had his office in the [[Coronet Capitol]],<ref name="SWG:StDO" /> in the [[Governmental Sector]] of [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]], on Corellia, and resided in the majestic thousand-year-old [[Corona House/Legends|Corona House]], the former residence of the [[King/Legends|monarchs]] of Corellia. In addition, the Diktat served as [[Governor-General/Legends|Governor-General]] of the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] and, consequently, as the [[Commander-in-Chief/Legends|commander-in-chief]] of the [[Corellian Defense Force/Legends|Corellian Defense Force]] and [[Director/Legends|director]] of the [[Corellian Security Force]]. Nevertheless, Gallamby was only a figure-head who only controlled the administration of the planet Corellia. Although he still held the title of Governor-General of the Corellian sector, the sector and its Corellian Defense Force were overseen by the Imperial [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of the Corellian sector. Furthermore, the Diktat had limited powers on Corellia where the boards of directors of the most powerful Corellian corporations controlled the government.<ref name="SoF" /> Working on boosting the planet's economy, they were likely the ones who plotted to assassinate Thomree, attempting to cut back on Corellia's support towards the Empire's [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="AaS" /> Yet, as the war escalated , the elements of the Corellian navy tasked with defending the Corellian sector were repeatedly sent away to support the Empire in dealing with Rebel activity. In turn, [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] turned their attention to the sector that became more dangerous for traders who began to avoid the area, thereby causing a decline of economy.<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref> The Diktat's response was to increased restrictions on freedom and encourage a restrictive isolationism, feeding—and perhaps also driven by—the streak of suspicion and distrust of outsiders in the Corellian sector. With the full support and protection of the Empire, Gallamby became an autocrat.<ref name="AaC">''[[Ambush at Corellia]]''</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Daclif Gallamby won a large and very lucrative contract with the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] for [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. But as the contract was tainted with illegality, the corrupted Diktat recruited a band of freelance [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to organize clandestinely the sale by meeting in the wilderness of Corellia the various people involved in the deal. The spacers were first sent to obtain the shipping order from CSA [[procurement officer]] [[Windle Meeker]]. They then delivered the approved order to the [[Transport Commission]] processing agent [[Merlyx Dolv]]. The latter was quite suspicious about the extra "administrative surcharge" included in the contract. The spacers were later ordered to collect a kickback from [[Dwynn Biuval]], a CEC sales representative, in a form of a donation to the [[Commission for the Rehabilitation of Unwanted Krahbu]], a charity administered by Gallamby. They then went to bribe [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mien Halloor]] of the [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs Offices]], that was assessing the shipment of certain CEC starships sold to the CSA. Thereafter, Merlyx Dolv, the agent of Transport Commission, contacted the office of the Diktat and threatened to cancel the sale of CEC freighters to the CSA. The spacers were immediately dispatched to capture Dolv and lead him to the Diktat. Merlyx Dolv was later assassinated. However, it soon appeared that Dolv had a friend who was aware of the situation and threatened to reveal the truth. Daclif Gallamby therefore hired the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] gangster [[Morgo One-Shot]] to eliminate Merlyx Dolv's friend. Afterward, the CEC-CSA deal was successfully concluded.<ref name="SWG:DG" />

Members of the Office of the Diktat included [[Thrackan Sal-Solo]], Gallamby's second in command;<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and [[Brantlee Spondoon]], the aide to the Diktat.<ref name="SWG:BS">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Brantlee Spondoon|Corellia}}</ref>

===The Rogue CorSec plot===
{{Quote|I do not have words to express the delight and deep thanks I have for your actions this day! I feared that the light had fallen from my administration, plunging the entire government into a darkness from which there would be no escape. But then out of that growing darkness, our hero arose. You!|Daclif Gallamby, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:RtToaGD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Receive the Thanks of a Grateful Diktat|Corellia}}</ref>}}
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Daclif Gallamby was the victim of a plot arranged by a group of former members of the Corellian Security Force known as the [[Rogue CorSec]].<ref name="SWG:GttSotRS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Go to the Source of the Remote Signal|Corellia}}</ref> Their objective was to create a public political-judicial scandal in order to overthrow the Diktat and his government.<ref name="SWG:RtToaGD" /> The Rogue CorSec first recruited the [[Ragtags]] to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] illegal [[Spice/Legends|spice]] in the Capitol building.<ref name="SWG:RtMSC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Reacquire the Missing Starship Component Shipment|Corellia}}</ref> Then, the Rogue CorSec planted a remote relay device directly on the Diktat's personal data terminal. With this device, they were able to send files from a remote location and make them appear as originating from the terminal to which the device was attached. The Rogue CorSec thus planted orders, apparently from the Diktat, instructing the Ragtags to smuggle spice into the capitol.<ref name="SWG:SOtGS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Seek Out the Gadget Specialist|Corellia}}</ref>

[[File:CoronetCapitol.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The [[Coronet Capitol]], headquarters of the Diktat]]
The plan was foiled because the Ragtags made a mistake and also smuggled in the Capitol a shipment stolen from the businessman [[Ral Mundi]].<ref name="SWG:RtMSC" /> In 1 ABY,<ref name="SWG_timeline" /> a spacer was recruited by CorSec [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mack Jasper]] to investigate the missing shipment of starship components ordered by the [[Ral Mundi's Starship Emporium]].<ref name="SWG:FtMSOSC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Find the Missing Shipment of Starship Components|Corellia}}</ref> The spacer discovered that the shipment was stolen by the Ragtags and transported into the Capitol. And even more strikingly, the spacer found the spice. The spacer reported to Lieutenant Jasper, who decided to investigate the case.<ref name="SWG:RtMSC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Reacquire the Missing Starship Component Shipment|Corellia}}</ref> The spacer infiltrated the Capitol and worked for [[Gallura Handerin]],<ref name="SWG:WftG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Working for the Government|Corellia}}</ref> as well as [[Brantlee Spondoon]], the aide to the Diktat.<ref name="SWG:HGtII">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Help Get the Informant's Information|Corellia}}</ref> During another mission, the spacer finally discovered the order from the Diktat to smuggle spice.<ref name="SWG:FowtRCAD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Find out what the Rogue CorSec Are Doing|Corellia}}</ref> Lieutenant Jasper thus ordered a search of the Diktat's office, and to the surprise of Gallamby, the spacer found the remote relay device.<ref name="SWG:StDO" /> A gadget specialist easily identified the device and helped Lieutenant Jasper to find the origin of the transmission.<ref name="SWG:SOtGS" /> The spacer tracked the signal to the [[Rogue CorSec Base]] and then infiltrated the base. The spacer downloaded proof of the Diktat's innocence in computers and finally killed the Rogue CorSec commanders who were responsible for the plot: [[Hiram Bish]] and [[Mtara Vinram]].<ref name="SWG:GttSotRS" /> When the spacer returned to the Coronet Capitol, the grateful Diktat Daclif Gallamby awarded the Corellian [[Medal for Distinguished Service]] to the spacer for the service rendered to himself and to the government.<ref name="SWG:RtToaGD" />

===Near ousting===
{{Quote|Gallamby was letting everything fall apart. Act of patriotism to try and kick ‘im out. And by the time of the battle of the second Death Star, I was almost ready to get rid of him. We were all set.|Thrackan Sal-Solo|<ref name="AaS" />}}
As the Galactic Civil War intensified after the Rebel victory at the Battle of Yavin, the Diktat and his government became highly paranoid<ref name="CTD" /> and terrified to be overthrown by the Rebellion or by their own population.<ref name="AaC" /> Eventually, the Corellian government enacted strict isolationist measures, restricting trade and traffic<ref name="CTD" /> throughout the entire sector. These measures caused the collapse of the Corellian economy.<ref name="AaC" />

Over the next years, the Corellian government's policy, focused on economy, began to shift away from that of the Emperor's, angering Thrackan Sal-Solo, who struggled to revert it. He began secretly plotting to overthrow Gallamby. Gathering support, Sal-Solo was ready to oust the Diktat in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], when news of the Empire's defeat, and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]'s death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] reached them. The [[Alien/Legends|non-human]] population of the Corellian System, viewing no Emperor as meaning no Empire, began to stand up to the Corellian government, disrupting Sal-Solo's plans and making him unable to remove Gallamby from office.<ref name="AaS" />

===Post-Endor===
{{Quote|Gallamby made a run for it. Him and his handlers. Took half the credits in the treasury with them.|Thrackan Sal-Solo|<ref name="AaS" />}}
After the Battle of Endor, Gallamby's regime remained loyal to the Empire.<ref name="Who's Who">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> Within six months of Endor, the Imperial Fleet deployment in the Corellian system had been increased to twenty-five [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]] under Admiral [[Jaeffis]]. This was one of the largest fleet deployments anywhere in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and it was due to be further strengthened when Admiral [[Roek]] arrived to assume overall command with the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Aggressor (Executor-class)|''Aggressor'']] and five other Star Destroyers. The Imperials' primary aim was to secure the vital [[CEC shipyards|orbital shipyards]].<ref name="R&R:TJtC">{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant}}</ref> In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Corellia briefly became the focus of the military power struggle within the Empire, when ''Aggressor'' defected to [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Josef Grunger]], who was building up his forces in preparation for an attempt to seize the Imperial throne. Another Grand Admiral, [[Danetta Pitta]], allied himself with Gallamby and took command of the loyalist forces in the sector, with a [[Grand Admiral Pitta's torpedo sphere|Torpedo Sphere]] as his command ship. The subsequent [[Battle of Tralus (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tralus]] decimated both fleets, however, and may have marked the end of any effective Imperial Navy presence in the sector.<ref name="Who's Who" />

Nevertheless, Diktat Gallamby remained in power, and subsequently continued his policy of inward-looking suspicion much as before.<ref name="XW:WG">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref> The Galactic Empire began to fragment after the Battle of Endor but Gallamby's position was strengthened at first by the massive arrival of Imperial officers and troops who sought to take refuge in the Corellian sector.<ref name="XW:RS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref> However, without the direct protection of the Emperor, the Diktat's government flickered as the resentful Corellian population protested vehemently against the regime.<ref name="CTD" /> Hence, the Diktat gradually lost power to the [[Sacorrian Triad]].<ref name="CatCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Gallamby reorganized the prestigious Corellian Security Force into the [[Public Safety Service]],<ref name="XW:WG" /> an Imperialistic security force and political police tasked with protecting the regime. Yet, the Diktat's restrictive measures seemed to have always been compromised at the practical level, the PSS being more concerned with appearances and petty disorder than with serious actions.<ref name="IJ">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>

Finally, sometime as early as [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]],<ref name="XW:SoA">''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]''</ref> the Corellian armed forces<ref name="AaS" /> and security forces eventually stopped protecting the government from the revolted population.<ref name="AaC" /> As his position became untenable, the Diktat and other leaders of his regime fled the Corellian system and retreated to the [[Outlier systems]] of the Corellian sector, taking half of the credits in the planet's treasury with them.<ref name="AaS" />

==Legacy==
{{Quote|I was the designated successor to the last Diktat under the Empire, before the usurpation by the so called New Republic. I am, therefore, the legitimate ruler of this sector, and I do here and now declare myself to be the legally appointed Diktat of Corellia.|Thrackan Sal-Solo during the [[Corellian Crisis]]|<ref name="AaC" />}}
With the sudden disappearance of the Diktat, his designated successor, Thrackan Sal-Solo, was not able to prevent the regime to collapse.<ref name="AaS" /> The Corellian administration remained in disarray until the Corellian sector was took over by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], which installed a Governor-General in the Diktat's stead.<ref name="CTD" />

In the next years, Imperial loyalists and supporters of Daclif Gallamby continued to exert influence on Corellia politics, and became the basis of the subsequent [[Human League]] created by Thrackan Sal-Solo.<ref name="AaC" /> In [[18 ABY/Legends|18 ABY]], during the [[Corellian Crisis]], the Corellian separatists of the Human League assassinated Governor-General [[Micamberlecto]] and seized control of Corellia. Thrackan Sal-Solo proclaimed himself the new Diktat of Corellia as the successor of Gallamby, with the objective to re-establish the previous regime.<ref name="AaS" /> However, Sal-Solo's reign was short-lived; he was arrested by the New Republic at the end of the Corellian Crisis.<ref name="CTD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The last Diktat of Corellia was first mentioned in the [[1995]] novel ''[[Ambush at Corellia]]'', the first book of the [[Star Wars: The Corellian Trilogy|Corellian Trilogy]] written by [[Roger MacBride Allen]].<ref name="AaC" /> The Diktat was identified as Daclif Gallamby in ''[[Assault at Selonia]]'', the second novel of the series.<ref name="AaS" />

Daclif Gallamby appeared a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] (NPC) in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:HGtII" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Daclif Gallamby was present in the original release of the game. Like most quest-giver NPCs in the earliest versions of the game, he proposed a series of "static quests" to players. These quests did not provide any experience and just consisted in moving to a random location in order to kill or protect one or several NPCs.<ref name="SWG:DG" /> Gallamby's static quests were eventually removed from the game with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> With this update, Daclif Gallamby was included in a series of more modern "dynamic quests," becoming one of the main protagonists of a new story arc, i.e., the Rogue CorSec plot against the Diktat.<ref name="SWG:StDO" /> This questline was later slightly revamped with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 27 Notes for February 15th, 2006|Publish 27]]," on [[February 15]], [[2006]],<ref name="SonyForumsCite-What's On Deck! Publish 28 Update Notes, April 26th">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/board/message?board.id=Announcements&message.id=3077&jump=true|text=What's On Deck! Publish 28 Update Notes, April 26th}}</ref> as the story arc was inserted into the "[[W:c:swg:Legacy Quest|Legacy Quest]]," the main questline in the game.<ref name="SWG:StDO" /><ref name="SWG:GttSotRS" /><ref name="SWG:RtToaGD" /><ref name="SWG:SOtGS" /><ref name="SWG:WftG" /><ref name="SWG:HGtII" /><ref name="SWG:FowtRCAD" />
